mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both positive MoM
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or the interaction term loses explanatory power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a high-inflation environment signals supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins and force negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns do not materialize in more than 50% of cases when INDPRO declines during high CPI periods, or the mechanism shows no predictive power outside the specified inflation regime.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level below 3.5% establishes an accommodative monetary baseline that reduces near-term discount rates and generates positive weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS falls below 3.5%, or the threshold effect disappears in a walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and stagnant total nonfarm payrolls indicates structural labor market weakness that suppresses consumer spending and drives negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.03, or the divergence pattern shows no statistical edge in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic momentum that translates into higher corporate revenues and positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of quarters with positive GDPC1 growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production during a period of rising interest rates signals demand destruction and margin pressure that triggers immediate negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns fail to materialize in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when FEDFUNDS is stable or declining.
